seoTitle: Manton vs Maranunga — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Manton and Maranunga. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Manton and Maranunga.
heading: Manton vs Maranunga — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Manton, Northern Territory and Maranunga, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Manton has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Maranunga (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Manton or Maranunga safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Manton has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Maranunga scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Manton scores 37/100 for bushfire risk. Maranunga scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Manton or Maranunga?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Manton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.92% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
